SEM Family for Structural Equation Models

Source: R/families.R
sem.Rd

Create a family object for structural equation models through the unified gllamm() interface. The data frame is passed as the first argument of gllamm(); the measurement and structural models live in the family object. See fit_sem.

Usage

sem(measurement, structural = NULL, missing = c("listwise", "fiml"), se = TRUE)

Arguments

measurement

Named list of one-sided indicator formulas

structural

Optional list of structural formulas (latent and/or observed predictors)

missing

"listwise" (default) or "fiml"

se

Compute standard errors (default TRUE)

Value

An object of class sem_family

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fit <- gllamm(d, family = sem(
  measurement = list(f1 = ~ x1 + x2 + x3, f2 = ~ y1 + y2 + y3),
  structural = list(f2 ~ f1)))
} # }
